segments.text: Do you know how to give an AI Agent high-signal context?
Next, I'll show you how Anthropic solves that problem.
This is dense, practical material to help you get better at using AI.
The lesson is longer, so follow and save it.
Anthropic treats context as a finite attention budget.
As the window grows, the next important signal can lose strength.
Long prompts can make an AI Agent chase details.
Use a six-part high-signal pack: outcome, rules, tools, examples, retrieval, and state.
Raw history fills the window and shrinks the goal.
Keep current constraints and live evidence inside.
Move the rest outside; nothing is deleted, and each visible token serves the action.
Retrieve details only for the next decision.
Each AI Agent step keeps four things visible: goal, constraints, evidence, and current state.
Everything else stays outside until its signal matters.

headlines.hook: DO YOU KNOW HOW TO GIVE AN AI AGENT HIGH-SIGNAL CONTEXT?
headlines.authority: AI AGENT CONTEXT IS A FINITE ATTENTION BUDGET
headlines.artifact: GIVE YOUR AI AGENT ONE SIX-PART SIGNAL PACK
headlines.budget: MORE INPUT DOES NOT MEAN MORE SIGNAL
headlines.decision: KEEP FOUR THINGS IN THE AI AGENT WINDOW

beats.visual: the cover question hard-cuts to one enlarged question object
one promise card names the Anthropic method
follow and save line receives one yellow emphasis
one evidence card states the finite-budget conclusion
one six-line context package pushes into focus
attention device begins at low signal
the same device switches to current signal
the same device reaches just-in-time retrieval
one decision card shows four retained fields
